title: Estepona Port Boat Rental Group: Costs, Boats & 2026 Guide
html:
Planning a group boat rental in Estepona Port? Chartering together is straightforward and affordable, with shared tours from €65 per person and private motorboats from €420 half-day. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators at Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, the main departure point, making it easy to compare options and book securely.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona Port for a group?

Group boat rental costs vary by boat type and season, with shared tours from €65 per person and private motorboats from €420 half-day. A full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests typically costs €1,200, €1,800. For a crewed yacht with skipper, plan from €600 half-day. For the best options, you can find a skippered charter that fits your group size and budget.

  • Shared group tour: €65, €90 per person, 3, 4 hours
  • Small motorboat (4, 6 guests) half-day: €420, €550
  • Catamaran (10, 12 guests) full-day: €1,200, €1,800
  • Crewed yacht (8, 10 guests) half-day: €600, €900

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

In Spain, you generally need a recreational licence for boats over 6m or with more than 15hp, but operators in Estepona Port often provide skippered charters so no licence is required. Always confirm with the operator: for bareboat rentals you may need the Spanish PER or an International Certificate of Competence (ICC). The operator will confirm exact requirements for your chosen boat.

Tip: If your group lacks a licensed skipper, book a crewed charter, the cost includes a local skipper who knows the best spots along the Costa del Sol, from Cabo de la Plata to the Sierra Bermeja views.

Where to set off in Estepona Port

Puerto Deportivo de Estepona is the primary marina for boat rentals, with direct access to the Mediterranean. From here, popular cruising grounds include the bay of Marbella and the upscale marina of Puerto Banús. For a quieter anchorage, head east towards Cala de la Dorada or west to the sandy beaches of San Luis de Sabinillas. A 20‑minute crossing brings you to La Sala del Moro, a spot known for clear water and lunch restaurants. Many charters also offer trips to the iconic rock of Gibraltar (about 15 nautical miles south) for a full‑day adventure.

Which boat to rent for your group

BoatHire24 lists a wide range of vessels to suit different group sizes and budgets. Here are four popular choices:

  1. Motorboat (6, 8 guests), ideal for a speedy half‑day trip, max 6 guests, from €420 half-day. Typical models include Axopar 24 or Ribcraft.
  2. Skippered Yacht (8 guests), a crewed sailing yacht for a relaxed cruise, max 8 guests, from €600 half-day. Look for a Sun Odyssey or Bavaria.
  3. Catamaran (12 guests), stable and spacious, perfect for parties or family groups, max 12 guests, from €1,200 full-day. Lagoon 42 or Bali 4.0 are common.
  4. Luxury Motor Yacht (10 guests), for a premium experience, consider an Azimut or Fairline, max 10 guests, from €900 half-day. For a specific model, see the Fairline for hire in Estepona Port.

At a glance

  • Price range: €65 per person shared to €1,800 full-day catamaran
  • Peak season: June to September (high season); May and October as shoulder months
  • Trip duration: 3, 4 hours (shared) to full-day (6, 8 hours)
  • Maximum capacity: up to 12 guests on most vessels
  • Currency: EUR

Booking is straightforward: select your date, group size, and boat type on BoatHire24, and pay securely online. For detailed guidance on deposits, cancellations, and what to bring, consult the booking FAQ. It covers everything from weather policies to catering options, so you and your group can plan with confidence.

Questions about boat rental in Estepona Port

Can we bring our own food and drink on the boat?

Most operators allow you to bring your own food and drinks, though some catamarans have a minimum spend for onboard catering; check the listing details before boarding.

What happens if the weather is bad on our booking day?

If the weather is unsafe, the operator will cancel and offer a full refund or reschedule; standard policies on BoatHire24 cover this scenario.

Is it possible to rent a boat for a stag or hen party in Estepona Port?

Yes, many boat owners accept group celebrations, but it is best to confirm in advance, as some skippers may have restrictions on loud music or glass.

How far in advance should we book for a group in 2026?

For peak months (July and August), booking at least 4, 6 weeks ahead is advisable; shoulder season (May and October) can be booked 2, 3 weeks ahead.

BoatHire24 remains the simplest way to compare verified boat listings and secure your booking in Estepona Port. If you own a boat, you can also list it on the platform to reach thousands of travellers; simply visit BoatHire24 to get started. Use the site to check availability for your preferred dates and group size, and begin planning your 2026 group adventure on the Mediterranean.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona Port and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
